description: Tender beef, carrots, and potatoes in a rich gravy. Easy, foolproof slow cooker method scaled for any size.
ingredients: [{"name": "chuck roast, trimmed or stew meat", "unit": "lbs", "amount": "1½–2"}, {"name": "all-purpose flour", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "vegetable oil", "unit": "teaspoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "medium onion, diced", "unit": "medium", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "garlic, minced or crushed", "unit": "cloves", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "low-sodium beef broth", "unit": "cup", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "tomato paste", "unit": "oz", "amount": "3"}, {"name": "red or Yukon Gold potatoes, cut into 1-inch chunks", "unit": "lb", "amount": "½"}, {"name": "carrots, peeled cut into ⅓ inch medallions", "unit": "units", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "frozen peas", "unit": "cup", "amount": "½"}, {"name": "low sodium soy sauce", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "salt", "unit": "teaspoon", "amount": "½"}, {"name": "pepper", "unit": "teaspoon", "amount": "¼"}, {"name": "bay leaves (optional)", "unit": "leaf", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "dried thyme (optional)", "unit": "teaspoon", "amount": "¼"}]
instructions: 1. Prepare meat and vegetables: Trim chuck roast into 1-inch cubes or use precut stew meat. Peel and cut carrots and potatoes. Dice onion and mince garlic. 2. Precook for flavor: Dust beef with flour and brown in hot oil over medium-high heat for 7-8 minutes. Move meat to crock pot. In same pan, cook onion with tomato paste and beef broth until onions soften, add garlic last minute. 3. Assemble and cook: Add all ingredients except peas to crock pot, stir well. Cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4 hours. 4. Finishing: Add frozen peas for last hour of cooking. Cook longer if needed until beef and potatoes are tender.
notes: Best beef is chuck roast or sirloin tip, cut and trimmed. Precut stew meat works too. Recipe fits a 3-4 quart crock pot; double for larger crock pot. Stovetop instructions available. Browning beef adds flavor but can be skipped. Leftovers keep 4 days refrigerated or 3 months frozen. Flavor improves after refrigeration.
tags: beef stew,crock pot,slow cooker,comfort food,main course,easy recipe
